message:
chore(beads): close ft-ds2ow

Sync close transition for empty-SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH fix in 26841c83.

Agent: RusticMeadow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-d70a9","title":"claude_code.auth.login_required lacks alternate anchor golden fixtures","description":"Rule: `claude_code.auth.login_required` in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:1705` ships four auth-flow anchors: `To sign in`, `login required`, `please authenticate`, and `auth required`.

Current corpus coverage under `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/corpus/claude_code/` only has one positive golden pair for the first phrasing:
- `login_required.txt`
- `login_required.expect.json`

There is a near-miss pair, but no committed positive fixture for the other shipped browser-auth phrasings. Add at least one alternate positive `.txt` + `.expect.json` golden exercising a non-`To sign in` variant so pack drift on login prompts is caught before the rule silently narrows.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:52:16.439731Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:22:20.553440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:22:20.553124Z","close_reason":"Added 3 alternate-anchor golden fixtures for claude_code.auth.login_required covering the remaining 3 of 4 shipped anchors beyond 'To sign in': login_required_v2 ('login required'), login_required_authenticate ('please authenticate'), login_required_auth_required ('auth required'). Each .txt contains a literal anchor from patterns.rs:1709-1714; each .expect.json mirrors the existing login_required.expect.json schema exactly with matched_text swapped for the covered anchor. Pack drift on any browser-auth phrasing now trips corpus_fixtures_match_expected.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dijpe","title":"connector_inbound_bridge: dedup uses attacker-controlled signal.timestamp_ms as 'now', lets replay bypass the window","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_inbound_bridge.rs:495` in `route_signal`:

```rust
if let Some(ref cid) = signal.correlation_id {
    let now_ms = signal.timestamp_ms;
    if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) {
        ...
    }
}
```

The bridge uses `signal.timestamp_ms` — explicitly documented at line 132 as 'Timestamp of the signal AT THE SOURCE' — as the 'now_ms' argument that drives dedup eviction. Every connector (github, slack, webhook, etc.) sends this field and the bridge trusts it as its clock.

## Concrete replay bypass

`SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record` calls `evict_expired(now_ms)` which pops entries where `now_ms - inserted > ttl_ms`. An attacker-controlled upstream can:

1. Send signal X with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=0` → inserted_at=0 in cache.
2. Send signal X again with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1` → evict_expired sees `now=ttl+1`, front-entry at inserted=0, `ttl+1 > ttl` → pops 'spam' from cache. Then check finds cache empty → inserts a new 'spam' entry → returns TRUE (treated as new). Dedup bypassed.

Repeat indefinitely by claiming monotonically-increasing timestamps. Every signal is seen as 'new' and routed to the pattern engine + EventBus, duplicating every downstream side effect (workflow triggers, storage writes, webhook cascades).

## Scope

Only bites the inbound bridge, whose callers ARE the external connectors (by design — this is the agent-owned ingest surface for webhook/API signals). A buggy connector that sends stale `timestamp_ms` values can unknowingly bypass dedup too; it doesn't require malice.

Outbound bridge doesn't have this shape — it uses its own `now_ms` from `SystemTime::now()` for all internal state.

## Fix

Drive dedup bookkeeping from the LOCAL system clock, not the signal's claimed timestamp. The signal.timestamp_ms field stays useful as analytics/telemetry data (end-to-end source→sink latency, etc.) but must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

Change `route_signal` to:

```rust
let now_ms = SystemTime::now()
    .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
    .unwrap_or_default()
    .as_millis() as u64;
if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) { ... }
```

## Regression test

- `dedup_rejects_replay_with_future_timestamp`: route X with cid='r' and timestamp=0, then route X with cid='r' and timestamp=ttl+1. Assert the second is still flagged `deduplicated=true` under a fresh bridge with a short ttl. Before the fix, the second call returns `deduplicated=false` because evict_expired sees now=ttl+1 and pops 'r'.

## Severity

MEDIUM — requires an external caller to either claim stale/future timestamps or replay signals. Doesn't crash. But every downstream connector action (Slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR comment) can be re-fired without bound by a single misbehaving connector.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:45:15.242201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.688022Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.687664Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0716e112. Replaced signal.timestamp_ms → SystemTime::now() in route_signal's dedup clock argument so an upstream claiming future timestamps can no longer evict live dedup entries and bypass the window. Regression test bridge_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_signal_timestamp (timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2 on replay → still flagged deduplicated=true) covers the attacker-replay shape. Verified: 44 connector_inbound_bridge:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dqsev","title":"[bug] delete_session runs 3 DELETEs without a transaction — partial failure yields orphaned checkpoints","description":"# Bug

\`session_restore::delete_session\` at \`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:1003\`:

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

Three independent DELETEs, each in its own implicit rusqlite
transaction. If step 1 succeeds but step 2 fails (lock contention,
disk full, IO error, cancellation), we leave the database in a
tornn-inconsistent state:

  - Option A: pane_states DELETED, checkpoints still present,
    session still present → \`load_checkpoint_by_id\` returns
    Some(checkpoint) but with empty \`pane_states\` → restore
    produces an empty-pane session that looks clean but carries no
    terminal state.

  - Option B: pane_states + checkpoints DELETED, session row
    still present → \`list_sessions\` shows the session, but
    \`load_latest_checkpoint\` returns None. Next \`delete_session\`
    call's third DELETE succeeds → session is eventually cleaned
    up, but the window is observable.

The other deletion-style paths in session_restore.rs DO wrap their
mutations in \`conn.transaction()\` (see lines 626, 673). delete_session
is the anomaly.

# Fix

Wrap the three DELETEs in a single \`conn.transaction()\` that
commits on success or rolls back on any intermediate error.

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let mut conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    let tx = conn.transaction()?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    tx.commit()?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

# Test

Regression test: inject mid-operation failure (e.g., by DELETING a
session that references a checkpoint whose pane_state rows have a
trigger or hook that fails). Without a transaction, the partial
state is observable; with the fix, the session row remains.

For a simpler test, assert that after a successful delete_session
there are zero rows in all three tables for the target session_id,
AND that commit/rollback atomicity holds across a wrapping PRAGMA.

# Scope

Single function, ~6 LOC change. No schema migration. Preserves the
return value contract (true if session row was deleted).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:06.907980Z","created_by":"pane3-delete-tx","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944931Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944608Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1a090629. Wrapped delete_session's three DELETE statements in a single conn.transaction() with commit on success. Pattern lifted from existing transactional helpers at session_restore.rs:626 + :673. Full cargo test blocked on ft-golgl pre-existing breakage — change is mechanical and preserves the return-value contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atomicity","session","storage"]}
-{"id":"ft-ds2ow","title":"[review][LOW] build.rs treats empty SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH as set, produces FT_BUILD_TS=epoch: and suppresses dirty check","description":"crates/frankenterm/build.rs:11 uses std::env::var("SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH").ok() — returns Ok("") when env var is exported empty (export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=), not Err(NotPresent). Rest of file treats Some("") same as Some("1700000000"): line 25 suppresses git-dirty check, line 43-44 match Some("") → format_epoch_utc("") → both date invocations fail → falls to "epoch:" literal. Downstream ft version --full shows `built: epoch:` which passes label-presence tests at main.rs:53702 but is wrong semantically. Spec says SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH MUST be non-negative integer. Fix: .ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()). Audit round 6.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:37.192815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:09.135648Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
+{"id":"ft-ds2ow","title":"[review][LOW] build.rs treats empty SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH as set, produces FT_BUILD_TS=epoch: and suppresses dirty check","description":"crates/frankenterm/build.rs:11 uses std::env::var("SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH").ok() — returns Ok("") when env var is exported empty (export SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=), not Err(NotPresent). Rest of file treats Some("") same as Some("1700000000"): line 25 suppresses git-dirty check, line 43-44 match Some("") → format_epoch_utc("") → both date invocations fail → falls to "epoch:" literal. Downstream ft version --full shows `built: epoch:` which passes label-presence tests at main.rs:53702 but is wrong semantically. Spec says SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH MUST be non-negative integer. Fix: .ok().filter(|s| !s.is_empty()). Audit round 6.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:32:37.192815Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:34:43.910506Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 26841c83: build.rs now uses .ok().filter(|s| !s.trim().is_empty()) so SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH='' and whitespace-only values collapse to None (fallback to wall-clock). Some('0') preserved as a valid epoch. Code comment documents the rationale. Build.rs has no cargo-test-reachable helpers, so no unit test added; verified via standalone rustc --edition 2024 parse + runtime dry-run on ['', '   ', '0', '1700000000', None].","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-e018k","title":"event_identity_key nested Object insertion order leaks into hash (recursive fix for 765743d5)","description":"765743d5 sorted top-level keys of extracted object, but nested Object/Array values went through serde_json::to_string which preserves IndexMap insertion order. Two logically-equivalent events with nested {'context': {'a':1,'b':2}} vs {'context': {'b':2,'a':1}} hashed differently → dedup-to-two. Fix landed in 50ff1eb0: canonicalize_json_value recursively sorts all nested Object keys before serialization (arrays stay ordered per JSON spec, but their contained Objects get canonicalized). Regression tests event_identity_key_ignores_nested_extracted_object_insertion_order + event_identity_key_canonicalizes_objects_inside_arrays.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:33.936724Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:25.227773Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:25.227512Z","close_reason":"Landed in 50ff1eb0 (fix(events): recursively canonicalize nested Object keys in normalized_extracted)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-e0ssu","title":"policy rate limiter global rejection creates empty per-pane buckets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:39:37.455407Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:42:19.194643Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:42:19.193768Z","close_reason":"Prevented global-limit rejections from allocating empty per-pane buckets, pruned empty stale pane buckets during check, and added a regression that repeated global rejections do not leak pane entries.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Mux client/pool/runtime migration completion","description":"Drive completion of DirectMuxClient/MuxPool/runtime-adjacent migrations and align semantics with core doctrine.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remaining runtime-incompatible primitives in mux client/pool paths are migrated or isolated with explicit adapters.\n2. Cancellation and backpressure semantics match doctrine and are documented.\n3. Unit tests cover mux path behavior under cancellation/retry/load.\n4. Integration tests validate core<->mux interactions after migration.\n5. E2E scripts verify mux reliability and structured diagnostics under stress.","notes":"This task should absorb and harmonize ongoing slices so we converge on one coherent mux runtime model. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:19.410169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:57.229937Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:57.229640Z","close_reason":"Sole child .2 closed - epic rollup complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mux","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2h5wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2449,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"SilverBluff starting a bounded 2026-03-22 support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is test-only: use the existing clean integration surface in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs to add any missing timeout-cascade and recovery-path assertions without touching the active mux_client.rs implementation work. Goal is to strengthen the mux migration verification lane while staying off adjacent in-flight source files.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:17:19Z"},{"id":2450,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"Progress 2026-03-22 (SilverBluff): landed a bounded test-only support slice in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Added two missing assertions on the clean integration surface without touching active mux_client.rs work: (1) pool_timeout_cascade_then_recovery_restores_capacity, which proves a timed-out nested acquire under capacity pressure does not leak permits or issue spurious mux reads and that the next healthy retry succeeds after release; (2) simulated_network_recovery_after_transient_fault_delivers_new_data, which proves a transient hostile-network fault can be followed by a healthy retry that delivers new payload bytes on the same underlying mock transport. Local hygiene checks are green: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs ✅ and git diff --check on that file ✅. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on vmi1153651 using: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-silverbluff-ft-e34d9-10-5-2 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored recovery -- --test-threads=1. Current remote state: real compilation is progressing (not in immediate source-error or stale state), but rch emitted the existing dependency-topology fail-open warning for sibling path deps ([RCH-E327], primary-root-only sync).,workdir:","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:26:55Z"},{"id":2451,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"Follow-up 2026-03-22 (SilverBluff): I cancelled rch build 29759189920253462 after ~9 minutes because it was still in a healthy first-compile dependency walk on vmi1153651 and I was no longer waiting on the session. Important distinction: no immediate source-level compile failure surfaced before cancellation; the run had progressed through substantial dependency compilation after the existing [RCH-E327] dependency-topology fail-open warning. This slice should be treated as code-complete but still awaiting a resumed targeted rch validation pass if fresh remote evidence is needed.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:28:18Z"},{"id":2544,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"JadeBeaver","text":"JadeBeaver bounded support slice 2026-03-27 under ft-e34d9.10.5.2: explicit-Cx cancellation was being ignored/misclassified at the runtime_compat semaphore + generic Pool boundary, which let vendored mux-pool explicit-Cx paths surface timeout semantics or proceed instead of failing as cancellation. Landed fix in runtime_compat.rs, pool.rs, vendored/mux_pool.rs, and a small downstream guard in wezterm.rs so PoolError::Cancelled does not trip the vendored circuit breaker. Added focused regressions for pre-cancelled pool/mux-pool operations and mid-wait permit cancellation, plus proptest coverage for the new PoolError variant. Local rustfmt --check and git diff --check are green. Remote evidence is in progress via valid offloaded runs 58601 and 77601; a separate run 66978 failed the remote dependency preflight with RCH-E324 and fell open local, so it was rejected and not used as validation evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:24:36Z"},{"id":2612,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Support slice ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2 completed on the clean integration harness only: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Added pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity, which proves a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held surfaces cancellation, preserves zero available permits, does not increment total_acquired, does not issue an extra mock mux request, and still allows the next healthy release/retry path to restore balance. Remote validation passed via rch on worker vmi1153651 with env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1 => 1 passed / 0 failed. Agent Mail is intermittently returning transient DB errors again, so this parent comment is the durable coordination record for the completion.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:32:05Z"},{"id":3574,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"cod1 support slice 2026-04-19: landed main commit 6a6f4ec5 on the clean integration harness only. Added spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs and wired matching structural/targeted coverage into tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_5_2_mux_migration_completion.sh. Local bash -n and git diff --check passed. A local cargo test lane was started with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cod1-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored spawn_with_cx_cancelled_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers -- --nocapture; it stayed in a real first-build dependency compile rather than surfacing a source failure before I moved on.","created_at":"2026-04-19T21:20:41Z"},{"id":3584,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"cod1 support slice 2026-04-19: added spawn_with_cx_timeout_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs and wired structural + targeted validation coverage into tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_5_2_mux_migration_completion.sh. Local bash -n and git diff --check passed. A targeted local cargo test lane was started with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cod1-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored spawn_with_cx_timeout_pool_acquire_fails_and_healthy_retry_recovers -- --nocapture; it remained in the real compile/link walk without surfacing a new source error before I checkpointed the slice.","created_at":"2026-04-19T22:24:59Z"},{"id":3593,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Support slice 2026-04-21: landed c1fb747e fix(vendored/mux_client): bind explicit-cx write timeout detail. The current vendored mux client already carried the explicit-Cx cancellation classification change; this follow-up fixes the remaining write-timeout match arm so timeout_with_cx passes the actual timeout detail into classify_cx_timeout instead of referring to an unbound name. Remote validation via rch surfaced this exact compile error in mux_client.rs, then also surfaced unrelated pre-existing frankenterm-core breakage in runtime.rs and mcp_tools.rs that prevented a full green run. rustfmt on mux_client.rs is currently blocked by a pre-existing parse-invalid PaneOutputSubscription block already present at HEAD around line 1891, so only git diff --check was used for local hygiene on this one-line delta.","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:54:25Z"},{"id":3594,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Pre-existing blocker tracked in ft-8jbpj.
